title: Cactus bihamatus

intro: The Cactus bihamatus, also known as the two-horned prickly pear, is a cactus species that belongs to the genus Opuntia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and diabetes, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cactus bihamatus include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, as well as polysaccharides and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cactus bihamatus is commonly prepared as an infusion or a salve, with the most common preparations being a tea made from the plant's pads and a topical gel or cream for wound healing.
